name: Credit Coalition Hecm-Hope
description_short: Non-profit credit counseling and homebuyer education organization in Houston providing free financial counseling, classes, and reverse mortgage guidance to low and moderate-income consumers.
description_long:
Credit Coalition is a Houston-based non-profit organization dedicated to empowering consumers through accessible financial and homebuyer counseling and education. Founded to serve community members seeking to enter or re-enter the credit mainstream, the organization has established itself as a trusted resource for individuals and families across all income levels, with particular focus on low and moderate-income households.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of free services including pre-purchase and financial education classes ("Fundamentals of Good Credit" and "Life As A Homeowner"), reverse mortgage counseling, one-on-one credit counseling, financial coaching, and community financial education. Classes are offered both in-person and online via Zoom, with bilingual options available (Spanish-language "Fundamentos Para Un Buen Crédito"). Their certificates of completion satisfy financial and homebuyer education requirements for multiple Texas housing assistance programs including TSAHC and TDHCA.

Credit Coalition distinguishes itself through its focus on actionable education rather than debt settlement or repair services. The organization provides curated resources on credit reports, fair housing, down payment assistance, home repair programs, foreclosure intervention, and Individual Development Account (IDA) programs specific to the Houston area. By offering free counseling and education without pursuing debt collection or settlement, they position themselves as advocates for consumer financial literacy and stability.

As a non-profit with board governance and community sponsors, Credit Coalition represents a legitimate free-help resource. However, their services are geographically limited to the Houston area, and detailed program outcomes or certification information beyond class offerings are not extensively documented on their public website. Prospective clients should verify current class schedules and specific eligibility requirements by contacting them directly.
pros: Completely free financial and homebuyer counseling and education classes
Bilingual programming with Spanish-language courses available
Certificates of completion satisfy requirements for multiple Texas housing programs (TSAHC, TDHCA, Cities of Houston, Baytown)
Offers specialized reverse mortgage counseling in addition to credit and homebuyer education
Both online (Zoom) and in-person class options for accessibility
One-on-one credit counseling and financial coaching available
Curated local resources specific to Houston area (IDA programs, home repair assistance, down payment help)
cons: Services limited to Houston area; not available nationwide or in other regions
Website lacks detailed information about counselor certifications, accreditations, or program outcomes
Limited information about wait times, availability, or how to schedule one-on-one counseling
No online screening tool or intake form visible on website to assess eligibility or match clients to programs
Reverse mortgage counseling details are minimal; unclear what specific HUD approval or certifications are held
best_for: Houston-area residents preparing to purchase a home for the first time
Low to moderate-income consumers rebuilding credit and financial literacy
Homeowners considering reverse mortgages who need unbiased counseling before proceeding
diagnosis: Credit Coalition is best for Houston-area residents seeking free, nonprofit credit counseling and homebuyer education without product sales or debt settlement pressure. The main caveat is geographic limitation to Houston; those outside this area will need to identify comparable HUD-approved counselors or NFCC-member agencies in their region.
services: Free "Fundamentals of Good Credit" classes (pre-purchase financial education)
Free "Life As A Homeowner" post-purchase education classes
Bilingual financial education (Spanish: "Fundamentos Para Un Buen Crédito")
Fair housing and legal issues information
Homebuyer education and credit information resources
Houston area IDA (Individual Development Account) program referrals
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
